things.name: La Boca & Caminito
things.description: Buenos Aires' most colourful neighbourhood — the birthplace of tango with brightly painted corrugated iron houses

things.name: Recoleta Cemetery
things.description: Monumental neoclassical necropolis housing Eva Perón's tomb — a city of the dead that rivals any museum

things.name: Tango Show & Dinner
things.description: Experience the sensual Argentine tango — Café Tortoni and El Viejo Almacen are among the finest venues

things.name: San Telmo Flea Market
things.description: Sunday antique market in the oldest barrio — street tango, antique shops and the covered mercado

things.name: Teatro Colón
things.description: One of the world's greatest opera houses — guided tours show off the seven-storey auditorium and workshops

things.name: Palermo Parks & Polo
things.description: Vast 400-hectare park system, rose garden, Japanese garden and the world's finest polo at Palermo fields

things.name: Puerto Madero Waterfront
things.description: Converted 19th-century port warehouses now housing Buenos Aires' finest restaurants and the Puente de la Mujer

things.name: Tigre Delta Day Trip
things.description: One-hour train to the Paraná river delta — boat tours through lush islands with riverside cafes and markets

things.name: Iguazú Falls
things.description: The world's greatest waterfall system — 1-hour flight for 275 falls wider than Niagara and Victoria combined

things.name: Argentine Asado Experience
things.description: A proper Argentine barbecue at a parrilla — bife de chorizo, morcilla and chimichurri sauce with Malbec wine
